accurate.why: All claims match the bill text (IRS rule title, Federal Register cite, no force/effect, April 10 2025 approval) and the CRA context is accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: Identifying the disapproval authority as the Congressional Review Act is correct context, not a fabrication; nothing is invented.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the resolution disapproves and nullifies the named IRS digital-asset broker reporting rule.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The summary correctly explains the rule is nullified and has no force or effect, accurately describing the legal change.

exceptions_conditions.why: The one-sentence disapproval resolution contains no material exceptions or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: The summary notes the April 10, 2025 approval date, the only relevant timing provision.

major_provisions_covered.why: The sole operative effect—nullification of the IRS rule—is captured.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is brief and free of padding.

correct_entities.why: The IRS, the rule title, the Congressional Review Act, and the Federal Register citation are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The Federal Register page (106928), publication date (Dec 30, 2024), and approval date (Apr 10, 2025) all match the bill.
